revisedPrompt:
Create exactly one PNG image: a 1024x512 pixel game-development pixel-art sprite sheet.

Use the attached reference image as the strict identity and style reference for the character Mito. Treat this as re-posing the same approved character, not redesigning him. Match the reference proportions, palette, pixel density, one-pixel dark outline, head-to-body ratio, sturdy body width, thick limbs, head size, hair shape, small low ponytail, vermilion hair ornament, white top, indigo-blue haori, young-leaf-green hakama, dark leggings with red ties, sandals, vermilion sash and tassel, pale-green magatama necklace, and the white ritual sword scabbard at the left hip.

Asset: animated sprite sheet, SNES 16-bit-plus pixel art, bright mythical-Japan action RPG, top-down 3/4 view, facing DOWN toward the viewer with face visible. Crisp low-resolution pixel clusters, no antialiasing blur, consistent palette, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors.

Canvas and layout: exact sheet size 1024x512px. Solid #FF00FF chroma-key background filling all empty space, perfectly flat, no gradients, no shadows, no texture. Grid is 4 columns x 2 rows with 8 equal 256x256px cells, but do NOT draw any grid lines, cell separator lines, borders, labels, frame numbers, text, or watermark. Top row frames 1-4 left to right; bottom row frames 5-8 left to right. Exactly one character per cell. Every frame must stay fully inside its own 256x256 cell with clear solid #FF00FF margin on all four sides.

Character scale lock: draw the character LARGE, filling most of each cell height with a small margin, but never touching cell edges. Every frame has the exact same character identity, same total upright body height, same head size, same head-to-body ratio, same sturdy body width, same limb thickness, same palette, same outline weight, same pixel density, same facing direction, and same scale. Feet share the same ground line in every cell, and the head stays at the same general height except for the specified 2-3px walk-cycle bob.

Sword/scabbard lock: the white sheathed short ritual sword stays at Mito's LEFT hip in every frame, attached to the body. It is a festival ritual implement, not a battle katana. Keep the scabbard the same length, angle, position relative to the torso, and distance from the hip across all 8 frames. It must never be held in the hand, never float away, never rotate, never lengthen or shorten, and never drift across frames.

Eight-frame walk cycle, one direction only, facing DOWN:
Frame 1: CONTACT A, Mito's LEFT foot planted forward, right foot back, planted foot flat under weight, arms counter-swing.
Frame 2: weight drop from frame 1, body 2-3 pixels lower, legs and arms visibly changed together, grounded feet.
Frame 3: PASSING pose, feet together under body, body at mid height, arms centered naturally.
Frame 4: push-off, body 2-3 pixels higher, legs and arms visibly changed together.
Frame 5: CONTACT B, Mito's RIGHT foot planted forward and left foot back, a mirror step of frame 1 with equal stride length, arms counter-swing opposite frame 1.
Frame 6: weight drop from frame 5, body 2-3 pixels lower, legs and arms visibly changed together, grounded feet.
Frame 7: PASSING pose, feet together under body, body at mid height.
Frame 8: push-off, body 2-3 pixels higher, legs and arms visibly changed together, looping cleanly back to frame 1.

Walk-cycle realism: the leading foot alternates correctly, left foot leads in frame 1 and right foot leads in frame 5. Arms swing opposite the legs. Arms and legs change pose together every frame. Both feet are visible and readable. No floating, tip-toeing, sliding, skating, or copied leg silhouettes. Consecutive frames must have visibly different leg silhouettes. Keep stride length even so the loop reads smoothly. Haori hem and vermilion tassels sway subtly with each step while character identity remains locked.

Cleanliness: no detached fragments, no isolated pixel islands, no dust specks, no leftover effect residue, no seam slivers, no extra objects, no companion creature. Every drawn pixel belongs to the single character in its own cell.

Avoid any recognizable third-party game character, logo, emblem, likeness, UI, watermark, or modern object. Avoid samurai armor, kabuto helmets, ninja clothing, two-sword daisho, guns, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, bird or companion creature, letters, and text.

Output must be exactly one clean 1024x512 PNG sprite sheet on solid #FF00FF background.
